args.desc: Total rows to attempt to generate
args.name: count

functions.doc: DELIBERATELY BROKEN: emits a batch tagged with a partition_id well above DuckDB's BATCH_INCREMENT=10^13 per-pipeline cap. C++ extension rejects at parse time.
functions.name: broken_batch_index_overflow

args.desc: Total rows to attempt to generate
args.name: count

functions.doc: DELIBERATELY BROKEN: declares supports_batch_index=True but emits a data batch with no vgi_batch_index metadata. C++ extension's contract check raises.
functions.name: broken_missing_batch_index_tag

functions.doc: DELIBERATELY BROKEN: declares partition_kind + partition-annotated field but emits a data batch without vgi_partition_values#b64 metadata. C++ extension's contract check raises.
functions.name: broken_missing_partition_values

args.desc: Total rows to attempt to generate
args.name: count

functions.doc: DELIBERATELY BROKEN: emits batches with strictly decreasing partition_id on one stream. C++ extension's monotonicity check raises (DuckDB's debug-only assertion is not relied upon).
functions.name: broken_non_monotone_batch_index

functions.doc: DELIBERATELY BROKEN: declares partition_kind on 'category' but emits a batch without 'category' AND doesn't supply an explicit partition_values override. The framework's auto-extract fails with RuntimeError before the wire.
functions.name: broken_partition_column_absent_from_batch

functions.doc: DELIBERATELY BROKEN: declares SINGLE_VALUE_PARTITIONS but supplies an explicit partition_values override with min != max. The framework's wrapper validation doesn't compare min vs max for SINGLE_VALUE; the C++ extension's defense-in-depth check in InstallBatch raises.
functions.name: broken_partition_min_neq_max

functions.doc: DELIBERATELY BROKEN: no field carries vgi.partition_column metadata (and partition_kind defaults to NOT_PARTITIONED), but the worker passes partition_values= on out.emit. The framework rejects with RuntimeError before the wire.
functions.name: broken_partition_values_no_annotation
